translated: "Now when the Pharisees saw that he and his disciples were eating without washing their hands ritually, they questioned him. For the Pharisees, and indeed all the Jews, cling to the tradition of the elders; unless they wash their hands ritually, they do not eat. And on coming from the marketplace they do not eat unless they purify themselves. There are many other things that they have received as traditions to keep, such as the washing of cups, pots, and bronze kettles.
